channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: GPU mining malware spreads via SEO poisoning, AI chatbots
item.description: Threat actors are targeting systems with high-performance computers in an ongoing cryptojacking campaign spread through a coordinated SEO poisoning operation that also manipulated AI chatbot recommend...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Ransomware Actors Show Up In Person to Steal Law Firm Data
item.description: The FBI warned that the extortion gang Silent Ransom Group is targeting law firms and socially engineering its way into servers and databases....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Latin American Cybercriminals Hoover Up Government Data
item.description: A purported leak exposing 5.8 million records of Uruguayan citizens is the latest incident where cybercriminals targeted government agencies to monetize citizen data....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: AI-Assisted Exploit Development Outpaces Scanner Detection
item.description: Attackers are using AI to dramatically reduce the time they need to develop a working exploit for a CVE, according to new research....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Grandoreiro Malware and BTMOB RAT Campaigns Target Windows and Android Users
item.description:
Latin America and Europe become the target of two banking trojan campaigns that are designed to infect Windows and Android devices with Grandoreiro and BTMOB malware, respectively.
That's according t...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Malicious npm Package Stole Files From Claude AI User Directory via GitHub
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a new malicious package on the npm registry that comes with information stealing capabilities.
According to OX Security, the package, named "mouse5212-super-...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Can you enforce strong Active Directory password rules without frustrating users?
item.description: Strong Active Directory passwords don't have to come at the expense of usability. Specops Software explains how passphrases, breached password protection, and self-service resets can improve security ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 5 Steps to Managing Shadow AI Tools Without Slowing Down Employees
item.description: When an employee installs an AI writing assistant, connects a coding copilot to their IDE, or starts summarizing meetings with a new browser tool, they are doing exactly what a productive employee sho...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Glassworm botnet disrupted after resilient C2 infrastructure takedown
item.description: The Glassworm botnet targeting developers in software supply-chain attacks has been disrupted after researchers took down its resilient command-and-control infrastructure relying on Solana blockchain ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Cybersecurity Evolution: How We Went From Perimeter Defense to AI-Native Security
item.description: The cybersecurity industry of 2006 barely resembled today's billion-dollar behemoth. As part of Dark Reading's 20th anniversary celebration, we trace the industry's evolution through a technology lens...
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: FBI warns of in-person data theft attacks from extortion gang
item.description: The FBI warned on Tuesday that the Silent Ransom Group (SRG) extortion gang is now targeting U.S.-based law firms in in-person data theft attacks. [...]...

item.title: GlassWorm Malware Takedown Disrupts Developer Supply Chain Attack Infrastructure
item.description: CrowdStrike, in partnership with Google and the Shadowserver Foundation, has announced the simultaneous disruption of all command-and-control (C2) channels associated with GlassWorm, a persistent soft...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: 3 SOC Steps that Shut Down Incident Risks Early
item.description: Most organizations still picture cyber defense as a fortress problem: build stronger walls, add more guards, buy another detection engine. But modern incidents rarely crash through the front gate. The...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Gitea Vulnerability Exposes Private Container Images without Authentication
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a security flaw in Gitea, an open-source, self-hosted platform for version control, that allows unauthenticated remote attackers to pull private container imag...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA gives feds 4 days to patch actively exploited cPanel plugin flaw
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has given U.S. federal agencies four days to secure their servers against a critical vulnerability in the LiteSpeed cPanel user-end plu...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Dutch police arrests suspect linked to Ajax football club hack
item.description: The Dutch National Police arrested a 35-year-old man suspected of hacking the professional football club Ajax Amsterdam (AFC Ajax) earlier this year. [...]...

item.title: Windows 11 KB5089573 update released with performance improvements
item.description: Microsoft has released the KB5089573 preview cumulative update for Windows 11 versions 25H2 and 24H2, which comes with 30 changes, including performance and reliability improvements. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: AI Chatbot Recommendations Redirect Users to Cryptojacking Malware Sites
item.description:
Microsoft has warned of an active cryptojacking campaign that makes use of artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot interactions as a mechanism for surfacing malicious download sites.
"This emerging deli...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: KnowledgeDeliver flaw exploited as a zero-day to install web shells
item.description: Hackers exploited a critical zero-day vulnerability in a server running the KnowledgeDeliver learning management system (LMS) to deploy the Godzilla web shell. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Feeding Frenzy: 'Megalodon' Malware Infects Thousands of GitHub Repos
item.description: In just six hours, the campaign quietly pushed thousands of malicious commits to more than 5,500 GitHub repositories, stealing credentials, developer secrets, and more....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Charter confirms data breach after ShinyHunters extortion threat
item.description: U.S. telecommunications giant Charter Communications has confirmed it suffered a data breach after the ShinyHunters extortion group threatened to leak stolen data unless a ransom is paid. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: State Cyber Leaders Beg Congress for More Funding, Support
item.description: A recent congressional hearing highlighted how states are reeling from federal cutbacks to important cyber grants and information sharing initiatives amid damaging attacks to critical infrastructure....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Shai-Hulud Hackers TeamPCP: Lucky or Skilled?
item.description: TeamPCP, the cybercrime group behind later waves of the Shai-Hulud worm, has done significant damage to the open source ecosystem. But it's not necessarily due to skill alone....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: For Enterprises, Security Remains Agentic AI's Biggest Challenge
item.description: Every company needs an agentic AI strategy, but the tools to allow agentic AI frameworks to be safely and securely adopted are just starting to appear....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft Issues Out-of-Band SharePoint Patch
item.description: SharePoint access often means access to the keys of the kingdom, something attackers and defenders understand all too well....

item.title: MuddyWater Uses DLL Side-Loading in Espionage Campaign Targeting 9 Countries
item.description:
The Iranian hacking group known as MuddyWater has been linked to a new campaign affecting at least nine organizations across nine countries on four continents in the first quarter of 2026.
The activi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: How Varonis Atlas integrates Claude Compliance API for AI governance
item.description: AI governance requires visibility into how AI tools interact with enterprise data. Varonis explains how its Atlas platform uses Claude Compliance API data to help monitor usage, investigate risk, and ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Defender can now automatically isolate hacked endpoints
item.description: Microsoft is testing a new Defender for Endpoint capability that will automatically isolate compromised endpoints to thwart attackers' attempts to move laterally across the network. [...]...
